title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: DEM stream network overlay.
code:
library(flooded) library(terra) #> terra 1.8.93 library(sf) #> Linking to GEOS 3.12.1, GDAL 3.8.4, PROJ 9.4.0; sf_use_s2() is TRUE  dem <- rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) slope <- rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE )  cat("DEM:", ncol(dem), "x", nrow(dem), "pixels at", res(dem)[1], "m
") #> DEM: 800 x 648 pixels at 10 m cat("Streams:", nrow(streams), "segments
") #> Streams: 50 segments cat("Upstream area range:", range(streams$upstream_area_ha), "ha
") #> Upstream area range: 1928.765 110337.4 ha cat("Mean annual precip range:", range(streams$map_upstream), "mm
") #> Mean annual precip range: 526 587 mm plot(dem, main = "Elevation (m)") plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1.5)

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: Burn stream network onto DEM grid using upstream contributing area cell value. VCA bankfull regression expects — estimates flood depth contributing area hectares. Rasterized streams coloured upstream area (ha).
code: stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") plot(stream_r, main = "Upstream area (ha)")

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: VCA bankfull regression precipitation term strongly controls flood depth: precip = 1 (default), precipitation term drops flood depths dramatically underestimated. Bulkley mainstem (upstream_area_ha ~ 110,000), difference ~2 m vs ~8 m flood depth. test data includes map_upstream — mean annual precipitation (mm) fwapg/ClimateBC, carried stream attribute. rasterize alongside streams create spatially varying precipitation surface stream cells.
code: bankfull_width = (upstream_area ^ 0.280) * 0.196 * (precip ^ 0.355) bankfull_depth = bankfull_width ^ 0.607 * 0.145 flood_depth    = bankfull_depth * flood_factor precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream") cat("Precip at stream cells:", range(values(precip_r), na.rm = TRUE), "mm\n") #> Precip at stream cells: 526 587 mm

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: VCA combines four spatial criteria. Let’s inspect one.

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: Cells slope <= 9% (VCA default) represent potentially flat valley floor. Slope mask: green = slope <= 9%.
code: mask_slope <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 9, operator = "<=") cat("Gentle slope:", sum(values(mask_slope) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "cells\n") #> Gentle slope: 280449 cells plot(mask_slope, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"), main = "Slope <= 9%",      legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 0.8)

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: Cells within 1000 m stream (half default max_width = 2000). Distance mask: corridor within 1 km streams.
code: mask_dist <- fl_mask_distance(stream_r, threshold = 1000) cat("Within 1 km:", sum(values(mask_dist) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "cells\n") #> Within 1 km: 288888 cells plot(mask_dist, col = c("grey90", "steelblue"), main = "Within 1 km of stream",      legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 0.8)

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: Accumulated cost (slope-weighted) distance streams. VCA default threshold 2500. Cost distance streams. Warm colours = high cost.
code: cost <- fl_cost_distance(slope, stream_r) mask_cost <- fl_mask(cost, threshold = 2500, operator = "<") cat("Cost < 2500:", sum(values(mask_cost) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "cells\n") #> Cost < 2500: 113534 cells plot(cost, main = "Cost distance", range = c(0, 5000)) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 0.8)

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: bankfull regression estimates flood depth upstream contributing area precipitation, interpolates water surface outward streams, identifies cells flood surface. Flood model: depth terrain (m).
code: flood <- fl_flood_model(dem, stream_r, flood_factor = 6, precip = precip_r,                         max_width = 2000) cat("Flooded cells:", sum(values(flood[["flooded"]]) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> Flooded cells: 62943 depth <- flood[["flood_depth"]] depth[depth == 0] <- NA plot(depth, main = "Flood depth (m)") plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 0.8)

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: fl_valley_confine() chains (slope + distance + cost + flood masks), applies morphological cleanup (closing, hole filling, small patch removal, majority filter), returns binary valley raster. Note precip argument — without , flood depths ~4x shallow resulting valley significantly narrower. Unconfined valleys (green) stream network.
code: valleys <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   slope_threshold = 9,   max_width = 2000,   cost_threshold = 2500,   flood_factor = 6,   precip = precip_r )  n_valley <- sum(values(valleys) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) cat("Valley cells:", n_valley, "/", ncell(valleys),     "(", round(100 * n_valley / ncell(valleys), 1), "%)\n") #> Valley cells: 55420 / 518400 ( 10.7 %) plot(valleys, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "Unconfined valleys", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1.2)

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: Keep valley patches touch stream cell — removes isolated flat areas disconnected network. Valley patches connected streams.
code: connected <- fl_patch_conn(valleys, stream_r) cat("Connected valley cells:",     sum(values(connected) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> Connected valley cells: 54487 plot(connected, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "Connected valleys", legend = FALSE) plot(st_geometry(streams), add = TRUE, col = "blue", lwd = 1.2)

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: fl_flood_trim() subtracts user-supplied masks. example, trim urban areas, steep terrain, railways, waterbodies. demonstrate removing cells slopes > 5% (stricter threshold).
code: steep_mask <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 5, operator = ">") trimmed <- fl_flood_trim(connected, steep_mask) cat("After trimming steep cells:",     sum(values(trimmed) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> After trimming steep cells: 37694

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: fl_flood_assemble() unions multiple binary rasters. useful combining outputs different flood models data sources.
code: # Example: union the connected valleys with a wider flood-only mask flooded_mask <- flood[["flooded"]] flooded_mask <- ifel(is.na(flooded_mask), 0L, flooded_mask) assembled <- fl_flood_assemble(connected, flooded_mask) cat("Assembled cells:", sum(values(assembled) == 1, na.rm = TRUE), "\n") #> Assembled cells: 64302

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah
text: VCA bankfull regression includes precipitation term (precip ^ 0.355) scales flood depth local climate. BC, map_upstream (mean annual precipitation mm) available stream attribute fwapg (Freshwater Atlas Postgres layer). jurisdictions, gridded precipitation product work — rasterize DEM grid pass precip argument. Omitting precipitation (precip = 1, default) underestimates flood depth roughly 4x wet climates (~500 mm MAP). produces valley half width correct result:
code: valleys_no_precip <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams, field = "upstream_area_ha",   precip = 1 )  n_no <- sum(values(valleys_no_precip) == 1, na.rm = TRUE) cat("Without precip:", n_no, "cells (",     round(100 * n_no / ncell(dem), 1), "%)\n") #> Without precip: 24840 cells ( 4.8 %) cat("With precip:   ", n_valley, "cells (",     round(100 * n_valley / ncell(dem), 1), "%)\n") #> With precip:    55420 cells ( 10.7 %)

title: Valley confinement on Neexdzii Kwah

code: @Manual{,   title = {flooded: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network},   author = {Allan Irvine},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/NewGraphEnvironment/flooded}, }

what: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
text: Portable floodplain delineation DEM stream network. Delineate unconfined valleys floodplain extent using Valley Confinement Algorithm (VCA) — combining slope thresholding, cost-distance analysis, flood surface modelling. Built terra R-native raster processing. Bring DEM streams; works anywhere.

title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
code: pak::pak("NewGraphEnvironment/flooded")

title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
text: See Valley confinement Neexdzii Kwah vignette full walkthrough bundled test data.
code: library(flooded)  dem <- terra::rast("dem.tif") streams <- sf::st_read("streams.gpkg")  # full pipeline — one call valleys <- fl_valley_confine(dem, streams, field = "upstream_area_ha")

title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
text: flood model uses VCA bankfull regression estimate flood depth stream cells: upstream_area (hectares) precip (mean annual precipitation, mm) important — omitting precipitation underestimates flood depth ~4x.
code: bankfull_width = (upstream_area ^ 0.280) * 0.196 * (precip ^ 0.355) bankfull_depth = bankfull_width ^ 0.607 * 0.145 flood_depth    = bankfull_depth * flood_factor

title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
text: Adapted : BlueGeo Devin Cairns (MIT) — Valley Confinement Algorithm USDA VCA Toolbox — original method bcfishpass Simon Norris (Apache 2.0) — lateral habitat assembly See LICENSE.note full attribution.

title: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network
text: MIT

what: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
text: Computes least-cost distance every cell nearest stream cell, accumulating friction (typically slope) along path. Stream cells seed points cost zero.

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
code: fl_cost_distance(friction, streams)

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
text: friction SpatRaster movement cost per cell (e.g., percent slope). Higher values = harder traverse. streams SpatRaster rasterized streams (output fl_stream_rasterize()). non-NA cell treated seed point.

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
text: SpatRaster accumulated cost distance. Stream cells value 0; cells increase cost-weighted distance nearest stream.

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
text: Uses terra::costDist() implements push-broom algorithm weighted distance. friction raster defines per-cell traversal cost streams identifies seed cells (cost = 0). Cells NA friction impassable barriers.

title: Accumulated cost distance from stream cells — fl_cost_distance
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") cost <- fl_cost_distance(slope, stream_r) terra::plot(cost, main = "Cost distance from streams", range = c(0, 5000))

title: Union multiple binary rasters — fl_flood_assemble
text: Combines multiple binary (0/1) rasters via logical . cell 1 1 input layer. Useful merging flood floodplain layers different sources.

title: Union multiple binary rasters — fl_flood_assemble
code: fl_flood_assemble(...)

title: Union multiple binary rasters — fl_flood_assemble
text: ... Two SpatRaster objects binary values, single multi-layer SpatRaster.

title: Union multiple binary rasters — fl_flood_assemble
text: SpatRaster 1 input 1, 0 otherwise.

title: Union multiple binary rasters — fl_flood_assemble
code: slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded"))  # Two masks with different thresholds gentle <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 5, operator = "<=") moderate <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 15, operator = "<=")  # Union: cells meeting either criterion combined <- fl_flood_assemble(gentle, moderate) terra::plot(combined, col = c("grey90", "steelblue"), main = "Assembled masks")

what: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
text: Takes flood surface elevation stream cells (fl_flood_surface()) interpolates outward produce continuous water surface, subtracts DEM get flood depth. Positive values indicate flooding.

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
code: fl_flood_depth(dem, flood_surface, max_width = 2000, streams = NULL)

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
text: dem SpatRaster elevation. flood_surface SpatRaster flood surface elevation stream cells (output fl_flood_surface()). NA non-stream cells. max_width Numeric. Maximum corridor width map units (metres) within interpolate. Default 2000 (1000m side). streams SpatRaster rasterized streams used define interpolation corridor. NULL, derived non-NA cells flood_surface.

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
text: SpatRaster flood depth (metres terrain). Positive values flooded; 0 stream cells; NA outside corridor depth negative (terrain flood surface).

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
text: Interpolation uses terra::interpIDW() (inverse distance weighting) propagate flood surface stream cells outward. differs Python VCA uses scipy.interpolate.griddata linear interpolation — IDW available natively terra produces similar results application. interpolation domain limited cells within max_width / 2 nearest stream cell avoid extrapolating distant terrain.

title: Interpolate flood surface and compute depth above terrain — fl_flood_depth
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream") surface <- fl_flood_surface(dem, stream_r, precip = precip_r) depth <- fl_flood_depth(dem, surface, max_width = 2000, streams = stream_r) terra::plot(depth, main = "Flood depth (m)")

what: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
text: Convenience wrapper calls fl_flood_surface() fl_flood_depth() sequence, returning multi-layer SpatRaster flood surface elevation, flood depth, binary flooded mask.

title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
code: fl_flood_model(dem, streams, flood_factor = 6, precip = 1, max_width = 2000)

title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
text: dem SpatRaster elevation. streams SpatRaster rasterized streams (output fl_stream_rasterize()). Cell values upstream contributing area hectares (another proxy channel size). flood_factor Numeric. Multiplier bankfull depth estimate flood depth. Default 6 (VCA convention). precip SpatRaster mean annual precipitation (mm), single numeric value applied uniformly. Default 1 (omits precipitation term). max_width Numeric. Maximum corridor width map units (metres) within interpolate. Default 2000 (1000m side).

title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
text: SpatRaster three layers: flood_surface Water surface elevation stream cells (NA elsewhere). flood_depth Depth terrain (metres). 0 streams, NA flooded. flooded Binary mask: 1 flood_depth > 0, 0 streams, NA elsewhere.

title: Run the full flood model — fl_flood_model
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream") flood <- fl_flood_model(dem, stream_r, flood_factor = 6, precip = precip_r)  # Three layers: flood_surface, flood_depth, flooded names(flood) #> [1] "flood_surface" "flood_depth"   "flooded"       terra::plot(flood[["flood_depth"]], main = "Flood depth (m)")

what: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
text: Estimates bankfull flood surface elevation stream cell using VCA bankfull regression, adds DEM elevation. result water surface elevation interpolated outward fl_flood_depth().

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
code: fl_flood_surface(dem, streams, flood_factor = 6, precip = 1)

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
text: dem SpatRaster elevation. streams SpatRaster rasterized streams (output fl_stream_rasterize()). Cell values upstream contributing area hectares (another proxy channel size). flood_factor Numeric. Multiplier bankfull depth estimate flood depth. Default 6 (VCA convention). precip SpatRaster mean annual precipitation (mm), single numeric value applied uniformly. Default 1 (omits precipitation term).

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
text: SpatRaster flood surface elevation stream cells NA elsewhere. grid dem.

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
text: Bankfull regressions follow Valley Confinement Algorithm:   precip = 1 (default), precipitation term drops flood depth depends contributing area. stream raster contains channel width instead contributing area, regression still produces relative flood surface — absolute depth differ spatial pattern preserved.
code: bankfull_width = (upstream_area ^ 0.280) * 0.196 * (precip ^ 0.355) bankfull_depth = bankfull_width ^ 0.607 * 0.145 flood_depth    = bankfull_depth * flood_factor flood_surface  = DEM + flood_depth

title: Compute flood surface elevation at stream cells — fl_flood_surface
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream")  # With precipitation — realistic flood surface surface <- fl_flood_surface(dem, stream_r, flood_factor = 6, precip = precip_r) terra::plot(surface, main = "Flood surface elevation (m)")

what: Subtract mask layers from a binary raster — fl_flood_trim
title: Subtract mask layers from a binary raster — fl_flood_trim
text: Removes (sets 0) cells binary raster overlap exclusion mask. Useful trimming floodplain urban areas, steep terrain, waterbodies, features.

title: Subtract mask layers from a binary raster — fl_flood_trim
code: fl_flood_trim(x, ...)

title: Subtract mask layers from a binary raster — fl_flood_trim
text: x SpatRaster binary values (0/1). ... One SpatRaster exclusion masks. Cells value 1 mask removed x.

title: Subtract mask layers from a binary raster — fl_flood_trim
text: SpatRaster grid x. Cells overlapping exclusion mask set 0.

title: Subtract mask layers from a binary raster — fl_flood_trim
code: slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) gentle <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 9, operator = "<=")  # Trim: remove cells on slopes > 5% (a stricter exclusion mask) steep <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 5, operator = ">") trimmed <- fl_flood_trim(gentle, steep) terra::plot(trimmed, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"), main = "Trimmed by steep slopes")

what: Create a binary mask from a raster by thresholding — fl_mask
title: Create a binary mask from a raster by thresholding — fl_mask
text: Applies comparison operator threshold raster, returning binary (0/1) mask. Useful slope masks, elevation masks, depth masks, etc.

title: Create a binary mask from a raster by thresholding — fl_mask
code: fl_mask(x, threshold, operator = "<=")

title: Create a binary mask from a raster by thresholding — fl_mask
text: x SpatRaster numeric values. threshold Numeric scalar. threshold value. operator Character. One "<=", "<", ">=", ">", "==", "!=". Default "<=".

title: Create a binary mask from a raster by thresholding — fl_mask
text: SpatRaster values 1 (condition met) 0 (condition met). NA cells x remain NA.

title: Create a binary mask from a raster by thresholding — fl_mask
code: slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) gentle <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 9, operator = "<=") terra::plot(gentle, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"), main = "Slope <= 9%")

what: Binary mask by Euclidean distance from features — fl_mask_distance
title: Binary mask by Euclidean distance from features — fl_mask_distance
text: Computes Euclidean distance non-NA cells raster returns binary mask distance within threshold. Useful constraining analysis corridor around streams (e.g., max_width parameter VCA).

title: Binary mask by Euclidean distance from features — fl_mask_distance
code: fl_mask_distance(x, threshold)

title: Binary mask by Euclidean distance from features — fl_mask_distance
text: x SpatRaster non-NA cells features measure distance (e.g., rasterized streams). threshold Numeric. Maximum distance map units (e.g., metres). Cells within distance 1; cells beyond 0.

title: Binary mask by Euclidean distance from features — fl_mask_distance
text: SpatRaster values 1 (within threshold) 0 (beyond).

title: Binary mask by Euclidean distance from features — fl_mask_distance
text: Distance computed terra::distance() calculates Euclidean distance nearest non-NA cell. Feature cells receive distance 0 always included mask.

title: Binary mask by Euclidean distance from features — fl_mask_distance
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") corridor <- fl_mask_distance(stream_r, threshold = 1000) terra::plot(corridor, col = c("grey90", "steelblue"), main = "Within 1 km of stream")

what: Keep only patches connected to anchor features — fl_patch_conn
title: Keep only patches connected to anchor features — fl_patch_conn
text: Identifies connected patches 1-valued cells keeps overlap anchor features (e.g., stream cells). Disconnected patches set 0.

title: Keep only patches connected to anchor features — fl_patch_conn
code: fl_patch_conn(x, anchor, directions = 4L)

title: Keep only patches connected to anchor features — fl_patch_conn
text: x SpatRaster binary values (0/1). anchor SpatRaster identifying anchor cells. non-NA, non-zero cell anchor (e.g., rasterized streams). directions Integer. 4 rook connectivity, 8 queen. Default 4.

title: Keep only patches connected to anchor features — fl_patch_conn
text: SpatRaster grid x. patches touching least one anchor cell retained.

title: Keep only patches connected to anchor features — fl_patch_conn
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") gentle <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 9, operator = "<=")  # Keep only gentle-slope patches that touch a stream connected <- fl_patch_conn(gentle, stream_r) terra::plot(connected, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"),      main = "Gentle slopes connected to streams")

what: Remove small patches from a binary raster — fl_patch_rm
title: Remove small patches from a binary raster — fl_patch_rm
text: Identifies connected patches 1-valued cells removes (sets 0) patch whose area size threshold.

title: Remove small patches from a binary raster — fl_patch_rm
code: fl_patch_rm(x, min_area, directions = 4L)

title: Remove small patches from a binary raster — fl_patch_rm
text: x SpatRaster binary values (0/1). min_area Numeric. Minimum patch area map units squared (e.g., m²). Patches smaller removed. directions Integer. 4 rook connectivity, 8 queen. Default 4 (matches VCA 4-connectivity convention).

title: Remove small patches from a binary raster — fl_patch_rm
text: SpatRaster grid x. Small patches set 0; values unchanged.

title: Remove small patches from a binary raster — fl_patch_rm
code: slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) gentle <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 9, operator = "<=")  # Remove patches smaller than 5000 m² cleaned <- fl_patch_rm(gentle, min_area = 5000) terra::plot(cleaned, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"), main = "Large gentle-slope patches")

what: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
text: Burns stream line features onto grid defined template raster. stream cell receives value field (typically upstream contributing area channel width); non-stream cells NA.

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
code: fl_stream_rasterize(streams, template, field = "channel_width")

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
text: streams sf linestring object stream network. template SpatRaster defines output grid (extent, resolution, CRS). Typically DEM. field Character. Column name streams use cell value. Must numeric. Default "channel_width".

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
text: SpatRaster grid template. Stream cells carry value field; cells NA.

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
text: Rasterization uses terra::rasterize() touches = FALSE (cells whose centre falls stream line burned). multiple features overlap cell, maximum value kept. output CRS matches template. streams template different CRS, streams reprojected match.

title: Rasterize a stream network onto a DEM grid — fl_stream_rasterize
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) stream_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "upstream_area_ha") terra::plot(stream_r, main = "Upstream area (ha)")

what: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: Orchestrates full VCA pipeline: slope thresholding, cost-distance analysis, flood surface modelling, morphological cleanup identify unconfined valley bottoms.

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
code: fl_valley_confine(   dem,   streams,   field = "channel_width",   slope = NULL,   slope_threshold = 9,   max_width = 2000,   cost_threshold = 2500,   flood_factor = 6,   precip = 1,   size_threshold = 5000,   hole_threshold = 2500 )

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: dem SpatRaster elevation. streams sf linestring object SpatRaster rasterized streams. sf, rasterized using field. field Character. Column name fl_stream_rasterize() streams sf. Default "channel_width". slope SpatRaster percent slope. NULL, derived dem. slope_threshold Numeric. Maximum percent slope valley floor. Default 9. max_width Numeric. Maximum valley width map units (metres). Default 2000. cost_threshold Numeric. Maximum accumulated cost distance. Default 2500. flood_factor Numeric. Multiplier bankfull depth. Default 6. precip SpatRaster numeric scalar precipitation. Default 1. size_threshold Numeric. Minimum valley patch area (m²). Default 5000. hole_threshold Numeric. Maximum hole area fill (m²). Default 2500.

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: SpatRaster binary values: 1 = unconfined valley, 0 = confined / hillslope, NA = outside analysis extent.

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
text: algorithm combines four criteria via intersection (): Slope mask — cells slope <= slope_threshold Distance mask — cells within max_width / 2 stream Cost distance mask — cells accumulated cost < cost_threshold Flood mask — cells identified flooded bankfull regression combined mask undergoes morphological cleanup: Closing filter (3x3) bridge small gaps Fill small holes (< hole_threshold) Remove small patches (< size_threshold) Majority filter (3x3) smooth edges Adapted USDA Valley Confinement Algorithm Toolbox (BlueGeo implementation Devin Cairns, MIT license) bcfishpass lateral habitat assembly (Simon Norris, Apache 2.0).

title: Delineate unconfined valleys using the Valley Confinement Algorithm — fl_valley_confine
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) streams <- sf::st_read(   system.file("testdata/streams.gpkg", package = "flooded"),   quiet = TRUE ) precip_r <- fl_stream_rasterize(streams, dem, field = "map_upstream")  valleys <- fl_valley_confine(   dem, streams,   field = "upstream_area_ha",   precip = precip_r ) terra::plot(valleys, col = c("grey90", "darkgreen"), main = "Unconfined valleys")

what: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
text: Polygonizes binary (0/1) valley raster, returning sf polygon layer. Valley cells (1) dissolved contiguous polygons; non-valley cells dropped. natural final step fl_valley_confine().

title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
code: fl_valley_poly(x, dissolve = TRUE)

title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
text: x SpatRaster binary values (0/1), typically output fl_valley_confine(). dissolve Logical. TRUE (default), adjacent valley cells dissolved multipolygon features. FALSE, contiguous patch separate polygon.

title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
text: sf polygon object CRS x. Contains valley column value 1.

title: Convert a binary valley raster to polygons — fl_valley_poly
code: dem <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/dem.tif", package = "flooded")) slope <- terra::rast(system.file("testdata/slope.tif", package = "flooded")) gentle <- fl_mask(slope, threshold = 9, operator = "<=") poly <- fl_valley_poly(gentle) terra::plot(dem, main = "Gentle slopes as polygons") plot(sf::st_geometry(poly), add = TRUE, col = "#00800040", border = "darkgreen")

what: flooded: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network — flooded-package
title: flooded: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network — flooded-package
text: Delineate floodplains digital elevation models stream networks using Valley Confinement Algorithm (VCA). Combines slope thresholding, cost-distance analysis, flood surface modelling identify unconfined valleys. Ported bcfishpass lateral habitat pipeline (Python) origins USDA VCA Toolbox BlueGeo.

title: flooded: Portable Floodplain Delineation from DEM and Stream Network — flooded-package
text: Maintainer: Allan Irvine al@newgraphenvironment.com (ORCID) contributors: Devin Cairns (Valley Confinement Algorithm (MIT license, Blue Geosimulation)) [copyright holder] Simon Norris (bcfishpass lateral habitat assembly (Apache 2.0)) [copyright holder] Canadian Wildlife Federation (bcfishpass (Apache 2.0)) [copyright holder] Province British Columbia (bcfishpass (Apache 2.0)) [copyright holder] New Graph Environment (bcfishpass (Apache 2.0)) [copyright holder]
